tasks.description: Create SQL migration to remove target_timeline_frame from duplicate_as_new_generation RPC. Drop the old 6-arg overload, create new 5-arg function without p_target_timeline_frame (remove variable declaration, remove the CASE branch at ~line 122 so it becomes: midpoint when p_next_timeline_frame IS NOT NULL, else p_timeline_frame + 30). Reapply GRANT EXECUTE and COMMENT on the new 5-arg signature. Wrap in BEGIN/COMMIT.

Update TypeScript types and callers for the RPC change. In src/shared/hooks/shots/useDuplicateAsNewGeneration.ts: remove target_timeline_frame from DuplicateAsNewGenerationInput interface (~line 20) and remove p_target_timeline_frame from the RPC call (~line 46). In src/shared/lib/timelinePositionCalculator.ts:74, update the comment to remove reference to target_timeline_frame. Check the test file src/shared/hooks/shots/__tests__/useDuplicateAsNewGeneration.test.ts to verify it doesn't assert p_target_timeline_frame (it uses expect.objectContaining so should pass, but verify).

Remove debug console.log statements. Remove three console.log calls from src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineEditor.tsx: (1) ~line 75 '[ShotCreate:resolve]' in resolveSelectedGenerationIdsForShotCreation(), (2) ~line 294 '[ShotCreate] handleCreateShotFromSelection', (3) ~line 303 '[ShotCreate] createShot result'. After removal, grep the entire codebase for '[ShotCreate' to confirm none remain anywhere.

Extract shared usePortalMousedownGuard hook and replace three duplicated workarounds. Create src/shared/hooks/usePortalMousedownGuard.ts with the shared useEffect pattern and thorough JSDoc explaining the root cause (createPortal renders into document.body, so Node.contains() returns false for portal content, but React synthetic events still bubble through React tree — this is by-design behavior, not a React 18 bug). Replace the duplicated useEffect blocks in: (1) src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/ClipAction.tsx (~lines 83-92), (2) src/shared/components/SelectionContextMenu.tsx (~lines 84-93), (3) src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineCanvas.tsx (~lines 288-297). Read each file first to understand the exact pattern and ref/dep signatures before writing the hook.

Simplify addImageToShot mutation to invalidation-only pattern. In src/shared/hooks/shots/useShotGenerationMutations.ts: (1) Remove onMutate entirely (no temp ID generation, no applyOptimisticCaches, no snapshot/rollback, no cancelShotsQueries/cancelShotGenerationsQuery). (2) Keep mutationFn as-is. (3) Simplify onSuccess to use enqueueGenerationsInvalidation with scope:'all', includeShots:true, includeProjectUnified:true, PLUS explicit invalidations for queryKeys.segments.liveTimeline(shot_id), queryKeys.segments.parents(shot_id, project_id), and queryKeys.generations.meta(shot_id). (4) Simplify onError: remove rollback logic, keep duplicate-key detection and error toast. (5) Remove AddImageToShotContext type. (6) Remove skipOptimistic from AddImageToShotVariables in addImageToShotHelpers.ts:20. (7) Update withUnpositionedAddImageVariables (~line 58-66) to remove skipOptimistic:true. (8) Search codebase for skipOptimistic and remove from any call sites. IMPORTANT: Before removing shared cache utilities (cancelShotsQueries, rollbackShotsCaches, etc.), verify useRemoveImageFromShot and useUpdateShotImageOrder don't still use them — only remove imports truly unused after this change.

Clean up addImageToShotHelpers.ts: remove now-unused functions and types. Remove: applyOptimisticCaches (~lines 187-227), replaceOptimisticItemInCache (~lines 242-270), replaceOptimisticItemInShotsCache (~lines 272-304), createOptimisticGenerationItem (~lines 154-185), resolveOptimisticFrame (~lines 137-152), CanonicalShotGenerationIdentity interface (~lines 23-26), withLegacyShotGenerationIdentity function (~lines 28-39). Remove imports that are only used by the removed functions: updateAllShotsCaches, calculateNextAvailableFrame, ensureUniqueFrame. Keep: runAddImageMutation, withVariableMetadata, toAddImageErrorMessage, AddImageToShotVariables, and the three insertion functions. Update imports in useShotGenerationMutations.ts to remove references to deleted exports. Update tests in src/shared/hooks/shots/__tests__/addImageToShotHelpers.test.ts: remove tests for the deleted functions, keep tests for the retained functions.

Run tests and verify all changes. (1) Run npx tsc --noEmit to catch type errors from all removed fields/parameters. (2) Run npx vitest run src/shared/hooks/shots/ to verify shot hook tests pass. (3) Run npx vitest run src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/ to verify timeline editor tests pass. (4) Grep for [ShotCreate to confirm no debug logs remain. (5) Grep for target_timeline_frame in src/ to confirm no TS references remain. (6) Grep for skipOptimistic to confirm no references remain. (7) Grep for replaceOptimisticItemInCache and applyOptimisticCaches to confirm no references remain. If any test fails, read the error, fix the code, and re-run until passing.

watch_items: FLAG-001: SQL migration MUST use DROP + CREATE (not CREATE OR REPLACE) because removing a parameter changes the PostgreSQL function signature. The old 6-arg overload would remain callable if only CREATE OR REPLACE is used.

FLAG-002: addImageToShot onSuccess MUST use invalidation-only, NOT direct setQueryData. runAddImageMutation returns raw shot_generations rows, not hydrated GenerationRow objects — direct cache writes would produce incomplete rows the UI can't render.

FLAG-003: Preserve queryKeys.segments.parents(shot_id, project_id) and queryKeys.segments.liveTimeline(shot_id) invalidations in the simplified addImageToShot onSuccess. enqueueGenerationsInvalidation does NOT cover these keys.

Before removing shared cache utilities (cancelShotsQueries, rollbackShotsCaches, findShotsCache, etc.) from imports, verify useRemoveImageFromShot and useUpdateShotImageOrder still use them. Only remove imports that are truly unused after the addImageToShot simplification.

The portal stopPropagation workaround is correct behavior, not a bug. createPortal renders into document.body so Node.contains() returns false. Do NOT attempt to 'fix' this by modifying click-outside listeners or walking React fiber internals.
Debt watch: Do not introduce any new optimistic temp ID patterns, render-time state mutations, or mock-only test coverage.

sense_checks.question: Does the SQL migration DROP the old 6-arg overload explicitly, CREATE the new 5-arg function, and reapply both GRANT EXECUTE and COMMENT on the new signature?
After removing target_timeline_frame from the TS interface and RPC call, does grep for 'target_timeline_frame' in src/ return zero results?
After removal, does grep for '[ShotCreate' across the entire codebase return zero results?
Does the new usePortalMousedownGuard hook have a JSDoc explaining the root cause (createPortal + Node.contains()), and do all three call sites now use the hook instead of inline useEffect blocks?
Does the simplified addImageToShot onSuccess include ALL THREE explicit invalidations (segments.liveTimeline, segments.parents, generations.meta) in addition to enqueueGenerationsInvalidation?

Are all five functions (applyOptimisticCaches, replaceOptimisticItemInCache, replaceOptimisticItemInShotsCache, createOptimisticGenerationItem, resolveOptimisticFrame) fully removed from addImageToShotHelpers.ts, and do their tests also get removed?
Does npx tsc --noEmit pass clean, and do all vitest runs in src/shared/hooks/shots/ and src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/ pass?

meta_commentary:
Four independent cleanup items. T1-T4 can all be executed in parallel since they touch completely different files. T5 and T6 are sequential (T6 cleans up helpers that T5 stops using). T7 validates everything at the end.

Key judgment calls:
- The SQL migration (T1) is the only item that touches production infrastructure. Review the migration SQL carefully before deploying with `npx supabase db push --linked`. Consider deploying it separately from the TS changes.
- For T5, the existing mutation has complex optimistic logic. Read the full current implementation before making changes — don't rely solely on line numbers from the plan as they may have shifted. The critical thing is matching the useDuplicateAsNewGeneration pattern (invalidation-only) while preserving the three segment/meta invalidations that enqueueGenerationsInvalidation doesn't cover.
- For T4, read all three workaround sites first to understand the exact ref and dependency signatures before designing the hook's API. The hook needs to be flexible enough for all three use cases.
- For T6, be conservative about what you remove. Grep for each function name before deleting to ensure nothing else imports it. The plan's line numbers are approximate.

plan_steps_covered.plan_step_summary: Step 1: Remove target_timeline_frame from RPC — SQL migration + TS types + callers + comment update + test check
Step 2: Remove debug console.log statements with [ShotCreate] tags
Step 3: Investigate Node.contains() portal issue and extract shared usePortalMousedownGuard hook
Step 4: Simplify addImageToShot mutation to invalidation-only pattern and clean up helpers
Step 5: Run tests and verify — vitest, tsc --noEmit, SQL deployment

validation.completeness_notes: All five plan steps are covered. T1+T2 split the RPC change into SQL and TS for cleaner review. T5+T6 split the mutation simplification into the hook rewrite and the helper cleanup for dependency ordering. SQL deployment (npx supabase db push --linked) is noted in T7 but should be done after human review of the migration.
